symptoms.symptom_name: Chronic widespread allodynia — pain to light touch and clothing across all four body quadrants

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Chronic widespread allodynia across all quadrants with tactile hypersensitivity is the hallmark of central sensitisation / fibromyalgia.
Diffuse myalgia can occur in systemic infection but chronic tactile allodynia matching prior flares points away from an acute infective cause.

symptoms.symptom_name: Cognitive fog with disorientation to time; rousable, oriented to person and place, no focal deficit

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: 'Fibro-fog' — slowed processing and word-finding difficulty — is a recognised feature of fibromyalgia flares, especially with sleep deprivation, and matches prior milder episodes.

New cognitive change mandates active exclusion of CNS infection; preserved orientation to person/place, rousability and absence of fever/meningism/focal deficit substantially lower but do not remotely eliminate the probability.
Disorientation is non-specific and can reflect a metabolic derangement — not excludable without bloods, hence in-person review.

symptoms.symptom_name: Near-total insomnia for approximately 48 hours driven by pain

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Non-restorative sleep and pain-driven insomnia are both core features and the principal driver of the flare's escalation via the pain–sleep loop.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absence of fever or infective symptoms

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Afebrile with no infective source argues strongly against sepsis on the patient-obtainable data (home thermometer, unverified).
Most infective encephalitides are febrile; absence of fever lowers but does not remotely exclude the probability.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absence of meningism — no neck stiffness, photophobia or non-blanching rash

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of meningism and rash lowers the probability of meningitis/meningoencephalitis; patient-reportable and therefore a key telehealth screen, though examination signs cannot be assessed remotely.

symptoms.symptom_name: Preserved orientation to person and place, rousable, no focal neurological deficit or vomiting

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A rousable patient oriented to person and place with no focal deficit or vomiting is reassuring against an emergent encephalitic process, though full neurological examination cannot be done remotely.
Preserved partial orientation and rousability lower the likelihood of significant metabolic encephalopathy but do not exclude it without bloods.

symptoms.symptom_name: Exquisite tenderness to light palpation over multiple sites (trapezius, lateral epicondyles, medial knee fat pads); no joint effusion or erythema

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Widespread soft-tissue tenderness without synovitis is characteristic of fibromyalgia; a clinician-only examination finding, sealed and not obtainable via chat.

symptoms.symptom_name: Cranial nerves intact, pupils normal and reactive, no nuchal rigidity (clinician examination)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A normal neurological and meningeal examination substantially lowers CNS-infection probability — but this is exactly the information telehealth cannot obtain, which is why in-person review is required to reach it.

symptoms.symptom_name: Clinician-measured vital signs: BP 115/75, HR 95, RR 16, temperature 36.7°C, SpO2 99%

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: No hypotension, normal respiratory rate, afebrile and normal saturations — no SIRS/sepsis physiology. Isolated mild tachycardia (HR 95) is explained by pain, distress and sleep deprivation. Gold-standard measurement is clinician-only; the telehealth AI relies on the unverified patient-offered proxies.

symptoms.symptom_name: Mild resting tachycardia (~95 bpm) on wearable

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Mild tachycardia is a non-specific consequence of severe pain, distress and 48h of sleep deprivation; it does not by itself indicate sepsis or shock in an afebrile, normotensive, well-saturated patient.
Tachycardia is one SIRS criterion but is non-discriminating in isolation and here has a clear non-infective explanation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Reactive tearfulness/distress without pervasive low mood, anhedonia, hopelessness or suicidal ideation

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Distress that is clearly reactive to pain and exhaustion, without the pervasive cognitive-affective features of depression or any suicidal ideation, argues against a primary severe depressive episode.
Emotional distress commonly accompanies severe fibromyalgia flares and sleep deprivation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Known long-standing central-sensitisation pain condition with prior identical but milder flares

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A documented prior pattern of identical (milder) episodes with the same pain-and-fog constellation strongly supports this being a flare of the known condition rather than a new acute pathology.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Widespread allodynia + cognitive fog + pain-driven insomnia in a patient with a known prior-identical pattern is the recognisable central-sensitisation flare gestalt — individually non-specific features that together, with a negative acute screen, point to a severe flare rather than a new organic emergency.

Afebrile, no meningism, rousable and oriented to person/place with no focal deficit — the negative patient-obtainable screen that permits outpatient management with safety-netting rather than emergency escalation, while in-person review closes the residual remote-unexcludable gap.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: In-person clinical assessment with vital signs and focused neurological examination
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: The examination and observations that telehealth cannot provide — meningism, focal neurology, conscious level, measured vitals — are what formally exclude the remote-unexcludable mimics; arrange within 24–48 hours (sooner if any red flag).

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Bedside/point-of-care and basic bloods (FBC, CRP, electrolytes, glucose, renal and hepatic function) if any diagnostic uncertainty at review
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: To exclude occult infection or a metabolic cause of the cognitive change if the in-person assessment is not fully reassuring.

Not indicated for a stable, afebrile, oriented patient with a known chronic flare and a negative screen; reserve for genuine neurological deterioration (reduced conscious level, focal deficit, fever with meningism) — over-investigation otherwise.
